content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

August 25, 1987

Honorable Lloyd G. Connelly
Member of the Assembly
State Capitol, Room 2176
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. I-87-231

Dear Lloyd:

Your letter of August 18 to Chairman Larson has been
referred to me for reponse.

You have asked for information concerning personal use of
campaign funds. I enclose copies of Elections Code Sections
12400-12405, which govern this issue. These statutes are not
part of the Political Reform Act,1/ the body of statutes which
the Commission is charged with administering and interpreting.
Therefore, we are unable to provide advice on their application.

I suggest that you contact the Attorney General's Office
with your questions on this topic. That office is responsible
for interpreting the personal use laws.

Please feel free to contact us again if we can provide any
assistance in the future.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]

                                        Diane M. Griffiths
                                        General Counsel

DMG:km
cc:  Chairman Larson

_______________

1/  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise noted.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

DISTRICT OFFICE
FORT SUTTER BUILDING
2705 K STREET, SUITE 6
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95816
443-1183

Assembly

California Legislature

CAPITOL OFFICE
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
445-2484

LLOYD G. CONNELLY
MEMBER OF THE LEGISLATURE
SIXTH ASSEMBLY DISTRICT

August 18, 1987

John Larson, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
488 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Mr. Larson:

Recently I have been contacted by a constituent who is concerned
about candidates' use of campaign monies for private trips and
paying the salaries of relatives. Would you please forward to me
copies of any statutes/regulations which pertain to this issue?
Any additional information which you believe would clarify my
understanding regarding this issue would be most appreciated as
well.

Thank you for your assistance.

Cordially,

LLOYD G. CONNELLY
Member of the Assembly

LGC:sgs

cc: Mrs. Mooney

sections.question: Recently I have been contacted by a constituent who is concerned about candidates' use of campaign monies for private trips and paying the salaries of relatives. Would you please forward to me copies of any statutes/regulations which pertain to this issue?
sections.conclusion: These statutes are not part of the Political Reform Act, the body of statutes which the Commission is charged with administering and interpreting. Therefore, we are unable to provide advice on their application. I suggest that you contact the Attorney General's Office with your questions on this topic. That office is responsible for interpreting the personal use laws.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the FPPC have guidance on the permissible use of campaign funds for private trips and paying relatives' salaries?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because personal use of campaign funds is governed by Elections Code sections 12400-12405, which fall outside the Political Reform Act that the FPPC administers. The FPPC referred the question to the Attorney General's Office, which has responsibility for interpreting the personal use laws.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request (Connelly's letter of August 18) and FPPC response (Griffiths' letter of August 25). Extracted question from incoming request and conclusion from FPPC response. No formal CONCLUSION section header, but the explanation of why advice cannot be provided serves as the substantive conclusion. File number 1-87-231 corresponds to document ID 87231.

CONCLUSION: These statutes are not part of the Political Reform Act, the body of statutes which the Commission is charged with administering and interpreting. Therefore, we are unable to provide advice on their application. I suggest that you contact the Attorney General's Office with your questions on this topic. That office is responsible for interpreting the personal use laws.

embedding.summary: Assemblymember Lloyd Connelly requested FPPC guidance on candidates' use of campaign funds for private trips and paying relatives' salaries. The FPPC declined the request, noting that these matters are governed by Elections Code sections outside its jurisdiction and referred him to the Attorney General's Office.
